title: nasturtium pyrenaicum subsp. hispanicum
intro: Nasturtium pyrenaicum subsp. hispanicum, a subspecies of the annual flowering plant Nasturtium pyrenaicum, is a type of flowering plant in the cress family, Brassicaceae. This plant, also known as Spanish tansy or Pyrenean cress, is used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Nasturtium pyrenaicum subsp. hispanicum include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate digestive problems. Traditionally, the plant's leaves and flowers are used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and salves, which are used to treat various health conditions.
